prompt: By De Morgan's laws, ¬(P ∨ Q) is equivalent to:
hint: Negation turns OR into AND and distributes onto each part.
explanation: De Morgan: ¬(P ∨ Q) ≡ ¬P ∧ ¬Q — 'neither P nor Q' means 'not P and not Q'. The mirror law is ¬(P ∧ Q) ≡ ¬P ∨ ¬Q. Note that ¬P ∨ ¬Q is the negation of the AND, not of the OR.
